zh.lifeSummary: Aldonça Rodrigues de Castro是Rodrigo FERNÁNDEZ DE CASTRO和Estevainha Peres的女兒，於1135年出生在西班牙艾尤斯卡迪維斯卡亞。她於1154年與Lope Díaz I de Haro在西班牙拉 利奧亞結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和7個女兒的父母。她去世於1214年2月16日在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安布爾戈斯享年79歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Aldonça Rodrigues de Castro是Rodrigo FERNÁNDEZ DE CASTRO和Estevainha Peres的女儿，于1135年出生在西班牙艾尤斯卡迪維斯卡亞。她于1154年与Lope Díaz I de Haro在西班牙拉 利奧亞结婚。他们至少是8个儿子和7个女儿的父母。她去世于1214年2月16日在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安布爾戈斯享年79岁。

nameMeanings.name: Rodrigues
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Jose, Carlos, Luis, Mario, Fernando, Alberto, Juan, Julio, Armando, Eduardo, Francisco.  Portuguese Joao, Agostinho, Paulo, Joaquim, Guilherme, Ilidio, Serafim, Vasco, Albano, Albeiro, Amadeu, Anabela.

Portuguese and Galician: patronymic from the personal name   Rodrigo  . This surname is also found in the cities of the west coast of India, having been taken there by Portuguese colonists. Compare   Rodrigue   2.
Spanish: variant or altered form of Rodríguez (see Rodriguez ).

variants.name: Rodriques
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Luis, Carlos, Fernando, Francisco, Gilberto, Juan, Adriano, Agusto, Alfredo, Alicia.  Portuguese Joao, Joaquim, Paulo.
Altered form of Portuguese Rodrigues and probably also of Spanish Rodríguez (see Rodriguez ). It is common in Brazil.

variants.name: Rodrigo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Elena, Rafael, Alfonso, Alicia, Angel, Araceli, Benardo, Carlos, Carlota, Cristina, Emilio.  Indian Devi, Dileep, Dilip, Gamini, Mahendra, Manoj, Sunil, Tilak.

Spanish and Portuguese: from the personal name Rodrigo, from ancient Germanic Hrōdrīc, a compound of hrōd ‘fame, renown’ + rīc ‘power, kingdom’. Rodrigo is one of the most important Spanish personal names of ancient Germanic origin. It was borne by the last of the Visigoth kings and by a Christian saint, Saint Roderick, one of the 9th-century martyrs of Córdoba. This surname is also found in and around some of the former Portuguese territories of western India.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Castro
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Manuel, Carlos, Luis, Jesus, Jorge, Miguel, Francisco, Mario, Pedro, Raul.  Portuguese Joao, Paulo, Ligia, Vasco, Wenceslao, Armanda, Fernandes.

Galician, Portuguese, and Jewish (Sephardic): topographic name from castro ‘castle, fortress’ (from Latin castrum ‘fort, Roman walled city’), or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word. See also De Castro .

Italian and Jewish (from Italy): topographic name for someone who lived by a fortification, from castro ‘fortification’ (see 1 above) or a habitational name for someone from the town of Castro, the center of a Duchy in Lazio (destroyed after its occupation by Papal military forces in 1649). Compare Lo Castro .

variants.name: Alcazar
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos, Jose, Francisco, Luis, Manuel, Juan, Julio, Rafael, Raul, Ruben, Alicia, Gustavo.

Spanish (Alcázar): habitational name from any of various places, for example in the provinces of Ciudad Real, Cuenca, and Granada, named with the word alcázar ‘citadel’ or ‘palace’ (from Arabic al ‘the’ + qaṣr ‘fortress’, a borrowing of Latin castrum; compare Castro ).

variants.name: De Castro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Luis, Armando, Mario, Alfredo, Jorge, Julio, Manuel, Ricardo, Rolando, Alvaro.
Galician and Portuguese: habitational name for someone from any of various places called with castro ‘castle, fortress’ (see Castro ), with the preposition de ‘of, from’.

variants.name: Castrillo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Jorge, Angel, Carlos, Emilio, Guillermo, Ramon, Alberto, Alejandro, Ana, Benito.
Spanish: habitational name from any of various places, especially in León, named Castrillo, from castrillo ‘fort’, a diminutive of Castro .

variants.name: Castronovo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Santo, Cosimo, Giacomo, Graziella, Sal, Stefano.
Italian (southern): habitational name from any of various places called Castronovo, from castro ‘fortification’ (see Castro ) + novo ‘new’.

historicEvents.description: The Christian territory controlled the Kingdoms of Aragon, Navarre, and Castile.

en.lifeSummary: Aldonça Rodrigues de Castro was born in 1135, in Biscay, Basque Country, Spain as the daughter of Rodrigo FERNÁNDEZ DE CASTRO and Estevainha Peres. She married Lope Díaz I de Haro in 1154, in La Rioja, Spain. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 7 daughters. She died on 16 February 1214, in Burgos, Castilla y León, Spain, at the age of 79.

fr.lifeSummary: Aldonça Rodrigues de Castro est née en 1135, à Biscaye, Euskadi, Espagne, fille de Rodrigo FERNÁNDEZ DE CASTRO et Estevainha Peres. Elle a épousé Lope Díaz I de Haro en 1154, à La Rioja, Espagne. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils et 7 filles. Elle est décédée le 16 février 1214, à Burgos, Castilla y León, Espagne, à l’âge de 79 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Aldonça Rodrigues de Castro wurde 1135 in Vizcaya, Euskadi, Spanien, als Tochter von Rodrigo FERNÁNDEZ DE CASTRO und Estevainha Peres geboren. Sie hat 1154 in La Rioja, Spanien Lope Díaz I de Haro geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 8 Söhne und 7 Töchter. Sie starb am 16. Februar 1214 im Alter von 79 Jahren in Burgos, Castilla y León, Spanien.

Lei sposato Lope Díaz I de Haro nel 1154, nel La Rioja, Spagna. Erano genitori di almeno 8 figli e 7 figlie. Morì il 16 febbraio 1214, nel Burgos, Castilla y León, Spagna, all'età di 79 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Aldonça Rodrigues de Castroは1135年にスペインエウスカジーヴィズケヤでRodrigo FERNÁNDEZ DE CASTROとEstevainha Peresの娘として生まれた。1154年にスペインラ リオジャでLope Díaz I de Haroと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも8人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼女は1214年2月16日にスペインカスティラ イ レoンブルゴスで79歳で死去した。

ko.Valladolid, Valladolid, Castilla y León,: 스페인 카스틸라 이 레o 발라도리드 발라도리드
ko.lifeSummary: Aldonça Rodrigues de Castro(은)는 1135년 스페인 우스카디 비즈카야에서 Rodrigo FERNÁNDEZ DE CASTRO(와)과 Estevainha Peres의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1154년 스페인 라 리오자에서 Lope Díaz I de Haro(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1214년 2월 16일 스페인 카스틸라 이 레o 버고스에서 79세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Aldonça Rodrigues de Castro nasceu em 1135, em Biscaia, Euskadi, Espanha filha de Rodrigo FERNÁNDEZ DE CASTRO e Estevainha Peres. Ela casou-se com Lope Díaz I de Haro em 1154, em La Rioja, Espanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 8 filhos e 7 filhas. Ela faleceu em 16 de fevereiro de 1214, em Burgos, Castilla y León, Espanha, com 79 anos.

ru.Valladolid, Valladolid, Castilla y León,: валладолид, валладолид, кастилла и леoн, Испания
ru.lifeSummary: Aldonça Rodrigues de Castro родилась в 1135 г. в местности визкеия, эускади, Испания. Её родителей звали Rodrigo FERNÁNDEZ DE CASTRO и Estevainha Peres. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Lope Díaz I de Haro в 1154 г. в местности ла риоджа, Испания. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 7 дочерей. Она умерла 16 февраля 1214 г. в местности бургос, кастилла и леoн, Испания в возрасте 79 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Aldonça Rodrigues de Castro nació en 1135, en Vizcaya, País Vasco, España como hija de Rodrigo FERNÁNDEZ DE CASTRO y Estevainha Peres. Se casó con Lope Díaz I de Haro en 1154, en La Rioja, España. Fueron padres de por lo menos 8 hijos y 7 hijas. Murió el 16 de febrero de 1214, en Burgos, Castilla y León, España, a la edad de 79 años.
